channel.title: Computer Science on Saleh Muhammed
channel.description: Recent content in Computer Science on Saleh Muhammed

item.title: .NET Notes in C#
item.description: Resoruces # Textbooks: Andrew Lock - ASP.NET Core in Action-Manning (2018). Concurrency in C# Cookbook: Asynchronous, Parallel, and Multithreaded Programming. LINQ in Action by Fabrice Marguerie, Steve Eichert, Jim Wooley. Websites: Npgsql Docs Speaking C# # Discover C# Compiler Version # With the C# 7.x generation, Microsoft decided to increase the cadence of language releases, releasing minor version numbers, also known as point releases, for the first time since C# 1.

item.title: Representations, Implementations and Algorithms of Graphs (and Trees)
item.description: Terminology # A graph \(G = (V, E)\) consists of a set of vertices \(V\) and a set of edges \(E\), such that each edge in \(E\) is a connection between a pair of vertices in \(V\)1 The number of vertices is written \(|V|\), and the number of edges is written \(|E|\). \(|E|\) can range from zero to a maximum of \(|V|^{2} - |V|\). A graph with relatively few edges is called sparse, while a graph with many edges is called dense.

item.title: Introduction to Non-theoretical Computational Geometry
item.description: Based on TopCoder’s thrive and Computational Geometry (252-1425-00L) HS13 lecture notes. (Problems mostly from LeetCode) Introduction # I’m not sure how to properly introduce this long plain text —or even how to put it—. You can easily consider it as a small handbook that contains some computational problems solutions, or like Nietzsche says in his Dawn of Day: “A DIGRESSION.—A book like this is not intended to be read through at once, or to be read aloud.

item.title: Dynamic Programming
item.description: Introduction # Follows: Roofgarden, T. (2019). Algorithms Illuminated (Part 3): Greedy Algorithms and Dynamic Programming (Illustrated ed.). Soundlikeyourself Publishing, LLC. Channel @ YouTube.com Objectives # Understanding functions (from memory perspective). Optimal Substructure. Overlapping Subproblems. Memoization. Understanding Dynamic Programming. Strategies for DP problems Solving DP problems. Memory And Functions # Consider the following C code: int g = 5; int main() { static int a; int b; int *p; p = (int*) malloc (sizeof(int)); *p = 10; } The process of compiling this file should look like this using a standard C compiler:

item.title: PostgreSQL Notes
item.description: Introduction into PostgreSQL # Notes: SQL commands is not case sensitive, therefore SELECT = select = SeLeCt Selecting # Regular Selection # Select syntax is being like this: SELECT*-- Everything FROMreal_person-- Table Name LIMIT10;-- Getting only they very first 5 IDs. This will print all email (s) from the customer table. Select distinct rows using distinct operator # DISTINCT clause is used to remove duplicates rows from the result output, it keeps only one row for each group if duplicated.

item.title: Problem Solving Tracking Map
item.description: Map # Problem in built directory are either not completed or wrong answers. The Main OJ is leetcode, all CF problems from the last reposotiery are removed. Problem Tags Difficulty Judge Statue 116. Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node Tree Medium LC Accepted 628. Maximum Product of Three Numbers Math Easy LC Accepted 268. Missing Number Math Easy LC Accepted 20.

item.title: STD Notes
item.description: C/STD Notes # Sorting # Sorting Custom Objects # You can sort array of custom objects using the std::sort like this: struct MyStruct { int key; std::string stringValue; MyStruct(int k, const std::string& s) : key(k), stringValue(s) {} }; struct less_than_key { inline bool operator() (const MyStruct& struct1, const MyStruct& struct2) { return (struct1.key < struct2.key); } }; std::vector < MyStruct > vec; vec.push_back(MyStruct(4, "test")); vec.push_back(MyStruct(3, "a")); vec.push_back(MyStruct(2, "is")); vec.push_back(MyStruct(1, "this")); std::sort(vec.
